businessSummary.oneLiner: World's largest EV manufacturer evolving into a 'Physical AI Platform' spanning autonomous vehicles, robots, energy, and AI chips
businessSummary.description: Tesla has expanded beyond electric vehicle sales to FSD autonomous driving, Cybercab robotaxis, Optimus humanoid robots, and Megapack energy storage. The company recorded $94.8B in annual revenue and $1.08 GAAP EPS for 2025, achieving a 20.1% gross margin in Q4 2025, the highest in two years. FSD has transitioned to a $99/month subscription model to maximize recurring revenue, with key catalysts including Cybercab production starting in April 2026 and Roadster unveiling.
howTheyMakeMoney: Energy storage and generation (Megapack, Powerwall, solar)
Services and charging (Supercharger network, insurance, OTA upgrades)

keyMetrics.name: P/E (TTM)
keyMetrics.interpretation: 15x higher than S&P 500 average (22x). Incomparable to automotive sector average (12x). Extreme premium reflecting EV+AI+robotics expectations

keyMetrics.name: Forward P/E
keyMetrics.interpretation: Based on 2026 expected EPS of $2.64. 6.6x higher than S&P 500 Forward P/E (21x). Extremely high growth expectations embedded

keyMetrics.name: Revenue Growth (YoY)
keyMetrics.interpretation: Negative growth deepening due to Q1 2026 delivery disappointment. Continued price cuts and intensifying competition

keyMetrics.name: Gross Margin
keyMetrics.interpretation: 2025 figure. Achieved 20.1% in Q4, showing improvement trend. Approaching automotive industry average (17%)

keyMetrics.name: Market Cap
keyMetrics.interpretation: Down 20% YTD. Q1 performance disappointment raises questions about Tesla's growth story

keyMetrics.name: 52-Week Range Position
keyMetrics.interpretation: 25% below 52-week high of $488.54. Still 24% above 52-week low of $295.33

growthDrivers.title: Cybercab Production Confirmed (April 2026, This Month)
growthDrivers.description: Financial Content confirmed: Cybercab production begins at Giga Texas in April. Fully autonomous robotaxi with no steering wheel or pedals. 247 Wall St. also reported confirmed 'April production start'

growthDrivers.title: Model X/S Discontinuation, All-In on Cybercab (4/3, TechCrunch)
growthDrivers.description: TechCrunch: The final days of Model X and S. Musk cancelled the $25,000 affordable EV plan and is betting everything on Cybercab and Optimus robots

growthDrivers.title: Technical Bounce Signals Emerging (4/4)
growthDrivers.description: RSI at 41.4 in neutral territory seeking rebound. MACD above signal line maintaining bullish momentum. Moving away from oversold conditions with potential technical bottom formation

growthDrivers.title: European Demand Recovery Continues
growthDrivers.description: France March registrations surged 203%, followed by 2x increases in Northern Europe. Despite Q1 disappointment, regional recovery signals remain clear

competitiveAdvantage.summary: Vertically integrated manufacturing, software OTA upgrades, Supercharger network, and FSD data advantage form core competitive strengths

moats.description: FSD: Over 1 billion miles of real-world driving data accumulated. Unmatched by competitors
50,000 Supercharger locations, North American standard (NACS) adoption makes competitors dependent
4680 battery, structural pack, gigapress optimize manufacturing costs
OTA upgrades continuously improve vehicle performance. Expanding subscription revenue

competitors.name: BYD
competitors.detail: China's #1 EV manufacturer. Superior price competitiveness but software disadvantage

competitors.name: Ford, GM
competitors.detail: Traditional OEMs. Transitioning to electrification but 2-3 years behind Tesla

competitors.name: Rivian, Lucid
competitors.detail: EV startups. Strong technology but lack production scale economies

competitors.name: Waymo, Cruise
competitors.detail: Autonomous driving specialists. Inferior to Tesla in data scale

recentNews.headline: TSLA Stock Down 20% YTD, Q1 Delivery Disappointment Continues
recentNews.significance: Q1 deliveries of 358,023 units fell short of market expectations, raising growth deceleration concerns. Production exceeded deliveries by 50,000 units, signaling demand softening

recentNews.headline: Model X/S Discontinuation Decision, All-In on Cybercab
recentNews.significance: Cancelled $25,000 affordable EV plan to focus on Cybercab and Optimus. Complete business model transition from traditional EVs to robotaxis

recentNews.headline: Tesla Records 2026's Largest Single-Day Drop
recentNews.significance: Q1 performance disappointment led to -5.4% single-day decline. YTD cumulative decline broke -20%, deteriorating investor sentiment

recentNews.headline: 247 Wall St: Tesla $1,000 Price Target, Betting on Autonomous Driving Boom
recentNews.significance: Successful Cybercab production could drive stock to $1,000. Autonomous vehicle market leadership is key growth driver

recentNews.headline: Q1 Earnings Release Scheduled for April 22
recentNews.significance: Tesla Q1 2026 financial results to be announced after market close. Focus on guidance and robotaxi updates

analystOpinions.notableComment: Stock Analysis 30-analyst average of $397.29. Extreme range of $125-$600 shows severe opinion divergence

risks.title: Q1 Performance Confirms Growth Deceleration
risks.description: 358,023 unit deliveries continue two-year negative growth trend. Production excess signals clear demand softening. Energy storage business also declined 38%

risks.title: Product Lineup Reduction with Model X/S Discontinuation
risks.description: Discontinuing existing premium models to bet everything on Cybercab. Risky all-or-nothing strategy with no alternatives if it fails

risks.title: 20% YTD Decline Deteriorates Investor Sentiment
risks.description: Sharp decline due to Q1 disappointment and growth concerns. Market cap reduced to $1.35T. Potential momentum investor exodus

risks.title: Intensifying Price Competition with Chinese EV Companies
risks.description: BYD, Li Auto expanding overseas creating price pressure. Rising margin compression risks. Declining China market share

buyReasons.title: Cybercab Production Ushers in Robotaxi Era
buyReasons.rationale: Confirmed April production start proves autonomous driving commercialization viability. Success could create entirely new business model. $1,000 price target possible

buyReasons.title: Attractive Entry Point After 20% Decline
buyReasons.rationale: Q1 disappointment and growth concerns excessively reflected in stock price. Attractive pricing for long-term investors

buyReasons.title: Analyst Price Target of $397 Offers 9% Upside
buyReasons.rationale: $32 cushion from current $365 to target. Recovery possible after near-term headwinds clear

buyReasons.title: Technical Rebound Signals Detected
buyReasons.rationale: RSI 41.4 neutral territory, bullish MACD. TradingView confirmed 2-hour chart bullish divergence. Entering oversold territory

overallOpinion: Q1 deliveries of 358,023 disappointed markets, driving YTD decline to -20%. Production exceeded deliveries by 50,000 units, confirming demand softening.
However, Cybercab production starting in April marks autonomous driving commercialization reality. Model X/S discontinued to go all-in on robotaxis.
Currently at $365 below both 50-day and 200-day averages, but RSI 41.4 seeks rebound. 9% upside to $397 analyst target remains.

investmentVerdict.summary: Short-term challenges from Q1 underperformance and growth concerns persist. However, Cybercab production represents a critical inflection point. While risky, success could create an entirely new business model. Technical rebound signals emerging suggest current holders should maintain positions while new investors should consider dollar-cost averaging strategies.

sources.description: Cybercab success could drive $1,000 price target

threads.hook: TSLA down 20%, but Cybercab production begins
threads.text:
$TSLA (4/4) $365.45(-20% YTD)

Q1 deliveries of 358,023 disappointed markets. Production exceeded deliveries by 50,000 units, confirming demand softening concerns. Model X/S discontinued to go all-in on Cybercab.

But April Cybercab production start is confirmed. Fully autonomous robotaxis without steering wheels or pedals. Risky bet, but success opens a new era.

RSI 41.4 neutral territory seeking rebound. $397 analyst target offers 9% upside. Hold for current owners, DCA for new investors.
